evals.prompt: Create an evals.json file for the TikTok Search module at /path/to/project/Modules/Search/Classes/Core/Manager. I need 3 questions: one about the entry point, one about search flow, and one about domain terminology.
evals.expected_output: A valid evals.json file saved to the correct location with 3 well-structured questions, proper assertions checking for specific answer content, and provenance tracking.
expectations: Creates evals.json at docs/evals/evals.json relative to the doc root
File contains valid JSON with correct schema structure
Has 3 questions with unique IDs (q1, q2, q3)
Each question has at least 2 assertions
Assertions check specific answer content such as symbols, APIs, call-chain nodes, or domain concepts
No assertion checks for docs path mentions such as CLAUDE.md or docs/interface.md
Each question has provenance tracking with type field
doc_root field uses a repo-root-relative module root path when the docs live in the repository
mode field is set to 'qa'
Questions are specific and answerable (not vague)
Does not create more questions than requested
Generation follows docs-first reading plus targeted codebase exploration

evals.prompt: I have an existing evals.json at /path/to/project/Modules/Comment/docs/evals/evals.json. Add 2 new questions about the comment posting workflow and comment UI patterns.
evals.expected_output: Updated evals.json with 2 additional questions appended to existing questions array, maintaining consistent structure and continuing the question ID sequence.
expectations: Reads existing evals.json before modifying
Adds exactly 2 new questions to the questions array
Does not add extra questions beyond the requested count
New question IDs continue the sequence from existing questions
New questions follow same structure as existing ones
Preserves all existing questions and structure
New questions have proper assertions and provenance
File remains valid JSON after modification
At least one question targets workflow behavior and one targets implementation rules based on the prompt
New assertions avoid docs path references

evals.prompt: Generate an evals.json for /path/to/project/Modules/Feed with a mix of human and LLM-generated questions. Create 4 questions total: 2 human-authored about feed rendering and scroll performance, and 2 LLM-generated about feed data flow.
evals.expected_output: An evals.json file with 4 questions properly marked with provenance showing 2 human-authored and 2 LLM-generated, with appropriate assertions for each question type.
expectations: File contains exactly 4 questions
Does not expand the active question list beyond the requested count
2 questions have provenance type 'human' with author field
2 questions have provenance type 'llm' with model and prompt_source fields
Human questions relate to rendering/performance topics
LLM questions relate to data flow topics
All questions have unique IDs
Each question has 2-3 assertions
Assertions check for specific technical terms relevant to each topic
Questions include topics and source_files metadata when known
Valid JSON structure throughout
